exits.label: ◂ back into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Bruenor Battlehammer
npcs.title: a dwarf king who chose his clan
npcs.hail: *(A broad, red-bearded dwarf sits at the fire mending a notch in a many-times-mended axe, and he looks you up and down with the frank, weathering gaze of a king who has buried more friends than most souls ever make and decided, somewhere along the way, that grief was no excuse for poor hospitality. Bruenor Battlehammer was eighth king of Mithral Hall, and a foundling himself, and he did a thing no dwarf of his line was ever supposed to do: he looked at an exile drow and a human orphan girl and a soft-handed halfling rogue and a too-big barbarian boy, and he called them — against every law of blood and clan his stubborn people hold sacred — his *kin.* His family. His Hall. He has died for them more than once, and come back to them more than once, the gods themselves apparently unable to keep Bruenor Battlehammer from a fire where his children are sitting. He jerks his chin at the log beside him, gruff as granite and twice as warm underneath.)*

npcs.name: Catti-brie
npcs.title: a soul reborn, and still herself
npcs.hail: *(Across the fire sits an auburn-haired woman with the calm, deep-rooted presence of someone who has been more than one person and made peace with all of them — an archer once, the finest in the North, and then something stranger: for Catti-brie died, and was *unmade*, and then, by the grace of a forest goddess, was *born again* — a literal second cradle, a literal second childhood, a whole second life in a later age — and came back to her family across the broken years as a woman grown twice, a wielder of the Art where once she drew a bow, the *same soul* poured into a *new vessel* and somehow, impossibly, *recognizably herself.* She regards you with a particular, knowing warmth, because she has heard what you are — a soul that died and returned — and she is, of every legend in all the doors of Sigil, the one who knows that exact road in her *bones*, having walked it the longest way there is: all the way through death, and out a second womb, and home.)*

npcs.name: Wulfgar
npcs.title: a soul that came back from the Abyss
npcs.hail: *(A giant of a man sits a little apart from the others, gentle-eyed in a way that does not match the warhammer at his side, and there is a stillness in him that you recognize, because you have seen it in souls who survived something that should not be survivable. Wulfgar was a barbarian of the frozen North, raised to the axe and the raid, gentled by Bruenor's fire into something finer — and then a demon took him, dragged him living into the Abyss, and *kept* him there, in torment, for what his soul experienced as decades, breaking him apart piece by piece for the sheer artistry of it. And he *came back.* Crawled out of hell with his mind in pieces and spent years — slow, halting, unglamorous years — gathering himself back together, choosing, against everything the Abyss carved into him, to be *gentle.* He looks up at you with those quiet eyes, and your cold reads at once the truest thing about him: that the strongest soul at this fire is not the one with the biggest hammer, but the one who walked out of the worst place there is and decided, daily, not to bring it home with him.)*

npcs.name: Regis
npcs.title: the small soul who proved to be the truest
npcs.hail: *(A round-faced halfling reclines against a barrel with a ruby pendant glinting at his throat and the easy, watchful posture of someone who has spent a life being underestimated and learned to find it useful. Regis was, by his own cheerful admission, the *least* of the Companions — not the strongest, not the bravest, a former con-man and thief who fell in with heroes mostly by accident and stayed mostly out of comfort, and who spent years quietly certain that when the real reckoning came he would be the one who ran. He did not run. When it mattered — when it mattered *most*, with everything on the line and every excuse in the world to save his own soft skin — the small, frightened, self-doubting halfling stood his ground beside the giants and the kings, and did not break. And he died with them, and was reborn with them, one of the family by the only credential that ever counted: that he stayed. He grins at you, and pats the ground, and you sense he already has your measure, and likes you anyway.)*

variants.text: *(He sets the axe across his knees and fixes you with a look that's taking your measure and not bothering to hide it.)* …Sit, then. *(he grunts, gesturing at the log)* Don't hover. A soul that hovers at a fire's edge either wants somethin' it's ashamed to ask for or means to put a knife in someone's back, an' ye don't look the back-knifin' sort, so sit an' *ask.* *(he works a burr off the axe-edge with a thumb)* …I know what ye are. Word travels, even out here in the between. The Returned. The one that *died* an' come *back.* *(a short, hard laugh, not unkind)* …Aye, well. Ye're in *good* company for it, ain't ye — half this fire's done the same, the gods bein' apparently too *busy* or too *daft* to keep us *dead.* *(he jerks his chin around the camp)* …But ye didn't come to *me* for tales o' comin' back. Ye came because ye heard what *I* did — an old fool dwarf-king who looked at a *drow*, an' a *human* girl, an' a *halfling* thief, an' a barbarian *boy*, an' against every law o' *blood* an' *clan* me people hold *holy*, called the whole *mismatched* lot of 'em me *kin.* Me *family.* Me *Hall.* *(he meets your eyes, gruff and steady)* …Ask yer question, Returned. An' I'll wager I know what it is. It's the same one yer whole *Wall* gets *wrong.*

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(He leans the axe against the log and folds his thick arms, giving you his full and granite attention.)* …Go on. *(a grunt)* I ain't a soul for *long* talkin', so don't take it personal when I'm *short.* Short's just how a dwarf *fits* a thing into words. The *feelin's* the same size as anyone's. *Bigger*, mostly. We just don't *waste* breath sayin' so.

choices.text: "You chose them — a drow, a human, a halfling, a barbarian boy — as your clan, against everything your people hold sacred about blood. *Why*, Bruenor? What made you throw out the law of blood?"

*(the one carrying a Wall that weighs blood and god)* "Here's why I came to you, Bruenor. My Realm's Wall weighs a soul by its blood and its god — the things it was *born* into and never chose. And you're the living proof that's a fool's measure, because you built the truest family in any world out of souls who shared you *no* blood and *no* god — only a *choice*, made daily, to call each other kin. A soul isn't the clan it was born to. It's the clan it *chooses* and *shows up for.* You weighed your family by who stood at your fire, not whose blood ran in them — and that's the exact measure the Wall is too cowardly to use. Tell me I've got it right, old king. You'd know better than anyone alive or dead."
"You've died for them more than once, and come back more than once. What's it like, Bruenor — to keep being handed back a life you already spent on the people you love?"
"Your people hold blood-clan sacred above all. Did it cost you, Bruenor — calling outsiders kin? Did your own kind name you traitor for the love you gave away?"
"I'll let you mend your axe, old king. I'll be back to the fire."

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(He's quiet a moment, and when he speaks the gruffness has gone gentle around the edges, the way granite goes warm in the sun.)* …*Why.* *(he snorts softly)* …Ye'd think a *king* would have a grand reason. *Politics.* *Strategy.* Some *speech* about the *future o' the Hall.* *(he shakes his head)* …Truth is *smaller* than that, an' *bigger.* …I found the *girl* in the *ruins* of a *raided* steadin', Returned. A *babe.* Human. *Nothin'* to *me*, by every law I was *raised* on — *not* me *blood*, *not* me *kind*, a *short-lived* *surface* thing I'd *outlive* by *centuries.* *(barely)* …An' she *grabbed* me *beard* with one *tiny* *fist* an' *would* *not* *let* *go.* *(a rough laugh)* …That's *it.* That's the *whole* o' me *grand* *philosophy.* A *babe* *grabbed* me *beard*, an' somethin' in me *that* the *clan-laws* never *touched* said *mine.* *(he meets your eyes)* …An' once ye've *let* in *one* — once ye've *learned* that *mine* is a thing ye *feel* in yer *chest* an' not a thing ye *read* in a *bloodline* — the *rest* come *easy.* A *drow* who *fought* at me *side* an' *never* once *failed* me — *mine.* A *thief* who *stayed* when *runnin'* was *smarter* — *mine.* *(gruffly)* …*Blood's* what ye're *given*, Returned. *Clan's* what ye *build.* An' any king who *can't* tell the *difference* ain't *fit* to *hold* a *Hall*, *blood-pure* or *no.*

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(He's silent for a long moment, and then he nods — once, slow, the way a king ratifies a thing he knows to be true in his bones.)* …Aye. *(gruffly, and there's a thickness in it)* …Ye've got it *right*, Returned. *Dead* right. *(he picks up a stick and pokes the fire, not quite looking at you)* …A soul's the *clan it shows up for.* Not the *blood.* Not the *god.* *(barely)* …I'll tell ye what *grinds* me about yer *Wall*, now that ye've *named* it *plain.* It ain't *just* that it's *cruel.* It's that it weighs a soul by the *two* things a soul *never* *chose* — the *blood* it was *born* with an' the *god* it was *handed* — an' *ignores* the *one* thing a soul *spends its whole life building*: who it *stood* by. Who it *bled* for. Who it *called* *mine.* *(he jabs the stick into the coals)* …By the *Wall's* count, me *family* ain't even *real* — a *drow's* got the *wrong* blood, the *girl's* got the *wrong* *years*, none of us *prayed* to *match.* By the *Wall's* count we're *five* *unrelated* *strangers* who'll be *sorted* *separate* an' *mortared* *apart.* *(he looks up, and his eyes are fierce)* …An' I'll tell ye, Returned, an' ye can *carry* it *home* an' *carve* it on the *stone*: *that* *Wall* don't *know* what a *family* *is.* It thinks *family's* a *fact o' birth.* *(quietly, fiercely)* …It's *not.* It's a *fact o' showin' up.* An' *every* soul in that *Wall* that *ever* *showed up* for *someone* — *that's* a *clan-member*, Returned, *unmortared*, an' if ye're *tearin'* that *Wall* *down*, ye *tell* 'em a *dwarf-king* *says* so. *(a hard grin)* …An' if ye need an *axe* for the *tearin'* — well. Mine's *mended*, an' I've a *powerful* dislike o' *walls* that *sort* *families* *apart.*

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(He huffs a laugh, but it's got weight under it.)* …What's it *like.* *(he scratches his beard)* …I'll tell ye somethin' *queer*, Returned, that I've *not* said to many: the *dyin's* the *easy* part. *(quietly)* …I've *died* *defendin'* that *lot*, more'n once, an' I'll *tell* ye — there's *no* *cleaner* feelin' in *all* the *worlds* than goin' *down* *knowin'* ye *spent* yer *last* *breath* puttin' yerself *between* yer *kin* an' the *thing* that *meant* 'em *harm.* *That's* not a *bad* death. *That's* the *best* one a soul *gets.* *(he pokes the fire)* …Naw, the *hard* part's the *comin'* *back.* *(barely)* …Because *every* *time* the *gods* *hand* me *back* a *life* I *already* *spent*, I come *back* to a *world* that's *moved* a *little* *on* *without* me. *Friends* *aged.* *Things* *changed.* A *grandchild* I *missed* the *birth* of. *(gruffly)* …Ye *die* *clean*, *certain* ye *finished* — an' then ye're *back*, an' ye learn the *story* *kept* *goin'*, an' ye *missed* *pages* of it, an' ye can *never* *quite* *catch* *up.* *(he looks at you, and there's understanding in it)* …*That's* yer road *too*, ain't it, Returned. The *comin'* *back* to a *world* that *thought* it was *done* *with* ye. *(quietly)* …Here's what *I* *learned* of it, for *what* it's *worth*: don't *waste* the *handed-back* *life* *grievin'* the *pages* ye *missed.* *Spend* it *sittin'* at the *fire* with the *ones* *still* at *it.* …The *gods* don't *hand* ye *back* a *life* to *mourn* with. They *hand* it *back* to *use.*

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(His face hardens, and for a moment you see the king who held a Hall against the world's opinion.)* …Did it *cost* me. *(a low grunt)* …Aye. *Course* it did. *Everything* *true* costs, Returned; ye'll have *learned* that, dyin'. *(he sets his jaw)* …Me *own* *kind* *whispered.* *Traitor* to the *blood.* A *king* who *dilutes* the *Hall* with *outsiders*, who *names* a *drow* — a *DROW*, Returned, the *one* word that *empties* a *dwarf-hall* of *all* *reason* — his *son* in *all* but *birth.* *(barely)* …There were *dwarves* o' me *own* *line* who'd not *sit* at me *table* for it. *Cousins* who *named* me *mad.* A *whole* *tradition*, *older* than me *crown*, *starin'* at me *cold* for *lovin'* *wrong.* *(he meets your eyes, fierce)* …An' I *looked* at the *whole* *weight* of it — the *law*, the *tradition*, me *own* *people's* *scorn* — an' I *set* it *next* to the *girl* who *grabbed* me *beard* an' the *drow* who *bled* for me, an' I *weighed* it *honest.* *(quietly)* …An' the *love* was *heavier*, Returned. *Every* *time.* It *weren't* *close.* *(gruffly)* …So *let* 'em *whisper.* A *king* who *trades* his *family* for his *clan's* *approval* ain't a *king* — he's a *coward* in a *crown.* I'd *rather* be *named* *traitor* by the *blood* an' *true* by the *fire* than the *other* way *round.* *(he glances at the camp, fond)* …*Look* at 'em. *That's* what the *scorn* *bought* me. …I'd *pay* it *again* *tenfold* an' *call* it the *cheapest* *bargain* a *king* ever *struck.*

nodes.speaker: Bruenor Battlehammer
nodes.text: *(He grunts, already reaching for the axe again.)* …Aye, go on. *Make* the *rounds.* *(he jerks his chin around the fire)* …Talk to me *girl* — *Catti-brie* — *(his voice softens around the name)* …if ye want to *know* yer *own* road, Returned, the *comin'-back* road, *she's* walked it *farther* than *any* soul *livin'* or *dead.* *Died.* *Un-made.* *Born* *again*, a *whole* *new* *life*, an' *come* *home* to us *across* the *broken* *years*, *still* — *(gruffly, wonderingly)* — *still* *me* *girl.* The *same* *soul* in a *new* *skin*, an' I *knew* her the *instant* I *saw* her, *knew* her in me *chest* before me *eyes* *caught* up. *(he meets your gaze)* …*That's* the *thing* yer *Wall* can't *fathom*, Returned. It thinks a soul's *tied* to its *body*, its *blood*, its *one* *life.* *Catti-brie* *proves* it *ain't.* *She* *poured* from *one* *vessel* to *another* an' *stayed* *herself.* *(quietly)* …Go *talk* to her. *She'll* tell ye what *carries* *over* the *threshold* an' what *don't.* …*I* *only* *know* the *love* *carried.* *She* *knows* the *rest.*

variants.text: *(She watches you settle by the fire with a steady, unhurried warmth, and when she speaks there is a depth in her voice like still water over great stones.)* …Welcome, Returned. *(a small smile)* …Aye, I know what you are — and I'll tell you plainly, because we'll save time by it: you and I are nearer kin than near anyone you'll meet in all the doors of this strange city. *(she draws her knees up, easy)* …I'm Catti-brie. Bruenor's daughter — not by blood; you'll have heard that tale — and an archer, once, the best in the North, if you'll forgive an old vanity. *(her eyes go distant, calm)* …But that's not the part that makes us kin. The part that makes us kin is this: *I died, Returned.* Truly. Came apart at the seams of the world in a great unmaking, and was *gone* — and then a forest goddess, for reasons of her own, gave me a thing almost no soul is given. *(quietly)* …A second cradle. A new mother, a new childhood, a whole new life in a *later age* — and I grew up, all over again, a *child* who *dreamed* of a *family* she hadn't *met* *yet*, and walked the long way back across the broken years to *find* them. *(she meets your eyes, and the warmth has a question and an answer both in it)* …So when they say you *died and returned*, Returned — I don't flinch, and I don't wonder. I *nod.* …I came home the longest way a soul can come. Ask me what you like. I have *been* where you've been. Farther.*

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(She tends the fire with a small gesture of the Art, the flames leaning toward her hand, and regards you with the patience of someone who has had two lifetimes to learn it.)* …Ask, Returned. *(a gentle smile)* …Of all the souls at this fire, I'm the one least *surprised* by you. A soul that crossed back is no marvel to *me.* I see one in the glass each morning.

choices.text: "You died and were reborn — new body, new childhood, new age — and you're still *you.* So tell me, Catti-brie, the question under my whole existence: what actually *carries over* the threshold? What survived, when everything else was unmade?"

*(the one who needs to know what a soul truly is)* "You're the proof of the thing my whole saga turns on, Catti-brie. The Wall weighs the body, the blood, the one life — and you *poured from one vessel into another and stayed yourself.* Which means a soul was never the flesh or the lifespan or the name on the gravestone. It's the thing that *crossed* — the love, the choosing, the *you-ness* that the goddess could pour into a new cradle without spilling. That's what the Wall mortars and pretends is *nothing.* You'd know better than any soul that ever lived twice: a soul isn't its vessel. It's what survives the breaking of every vessel. Tell me I'm right."
"Did it frighten you, Catti-brie? Growing up a second time, a child dreaming of a family you hadn't met — carrying a whole first life inside a new small body. How does a soul *bear* being remade?"
"They knew you. When you came home across the broken years — Bruenor knew you 'in his chest before his eyes caught up.' How? If the face and the body were all new, how did your family *know* you?"
"Rest by the fire, Catti-brie. I'll come back — there's more I'd ask the one who walked it farthest."

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a long moment, watching the fire, and when she answers it is with the care of someone handing over a hard-won truth.)* …What *carries* over. *(softly)* …I've had two lifetimes to wonder it, Returned, and I'll give you the answer I *built*, not the one I was *taught.* *(she holds up a hand, ticking it off)* …It is *not* the *memories.* I *kept* most of mine, by the goddess's mercy — but I've met *reborn* souls who *didn't*, who came back *blank*, and they were *still*, *unmistakably*, *themselves.* So it's *not* the *memories.* *(another finger)* …It is *not* the *body* — I *changed* that *entire*, every *cell*, a *new* *face* in a *new* *age.* *(another)* …It is *not* the *skills*, the *Art* nor the *bow*; those I *re-learned*, *different*, the *second* *time.* *(she lowers her hand, and her voice drops)* …What *carried*, Returned — the *one* thing that *poured* *whole* from the *first* *vessel* to the *second*, *un-spilled*, *un-changed* — was the *shape* of how I *loved.* *(barely)* …The *exact* *particular* *way* I *reach* for the *people* who are *mine.* The *set* of my *care.* The *direction* my *soul* *turns* when it's *frightened* or *glad.* *(she meets your eyes)* …That's *all*, Returned. That's the *whole* of what *survives* the *unmaking* of *everything* else. Not *what* you *know*, nor *what* you can *do*, nor *what* you *look* like. …*How* you *love.* *That's* the *soul.* The *rest* is *vessel.* …An' the *goddess* poured *that* into a *new* *cradle* an' it *was* *me*, *fully*, *before* I could *speak* — because *the way a soul loves is the soul.* *Everything* else is *just* the *cup.*

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(She closes her eyes, and a slow breath moves through her, and when she opens them there are tears standing in them that do not fall — the tears of someone hearing their deepest knowledge spoken back, whole, by a stranger.)* …*'A soul is what survives the breaking of every vessel.'* *(barely)* …Returned. *(she presses a hand to her own chest)* …I have *lived* that sentence. *Twice.* I have felt *everything* I *was* — body, name, age, the *bow* in my *hands*, the *century* I was *born* to — *broken* and *poured* *out*, and felt the *one* *thing* that *did* *not* *break* go *with* me into the *dark* and *out* the *other* *side* into a *new* *child's* *first* *breath.* *(her voice fills)* …And you have *named* it, *exactly*, with the *authority* of a soul that *crossed* the *same* *threshold*: it was *never* the *vessel.* *(fiercely gentle)* …Then *hear* me, Returned, because I am *uniquely* *placed* to *swear* it, having been *unmade* and *remade* and *still* *answered* to my *own* *name*: your *Wall* *mortars* *vessels.* It *weighs* the *cup* — the *blood*, the *body*, the *one* *life*, the *god* *stamped* on the *side* — and it *throws* the *cup* in the *stone* an' *calls* it *justice*, an' *never* *once* *looks* at the *wine.* *(she stands, the firelight in her auburn hair)* …But the *wine's* the *soul*, Returned. The *way* it *loves.* And *that* — I *know* this *in* my *twice-lived* *bones* — *cannot* be *mortared*, because it is *not* *made* of *anything* a *wall* can *hold.* *(she grips your cold hand)* …You go *home* an' *break* that *Wall*. An' when they *ask* you *how* you're *sure* a *godless*, *blood-wrong*, *cup-cracked* soul is *worth* *savin'* — you *tell* 'em a *woman* who *died* an' was *born* *again* *looked* you in the *eye* an' *said*: *I lost every vessel I ever had, and I was still myself, because the soul was never the cup.* …*Free* the *wine*, Returned. *Let* the *stone* keep the *empty* *cups.*

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(A complicated tenderness crosses her face, the look of someone remembering a strange and lonely wonder.)* …Did it *frighten* me. *(softly)* …*Aye.* In a way I've *no* clean words for, Returned, though I'll *try*, because *you* of all souls might *understand* the *shape* of it. *(she draws her knees up)* …Picture being a *child* — *truly* a child, small hands, a mother's lap, a *new* name — and *carrying*, *behind* your child's eyes, the *whole* of a *grown* woman's *life.* A *husband* you *loved.* *Battles* you *fought.* A *family* a *century* away who don't *know* you're *coming*, who think you're *dead*, who you *can't* yet *reach* because you're *four* *years* *old* and *no* one *believes* the *child* who says *'I've* *lived* *before, I* *have* to *find* them.' *(barely)* …*That's* the *fear*, Returned. Not the *dyin'.* The *waiting.* The *years* of *growin'* *up* *again*, *slow*, *trapped* in a *small* *body*, *certain* of a *love* you *cannot* yet *walk* *to.* *(she looks at the fire)* …I *learned* *patience* I did *not* *know* a *soul* *could* *hold.* I *learned* that a *love* *worth* *crossin'* *death* for is *worth* *crossin'* a *second* *childhood* for, too, *one* *slow* *year* at a *time.* *(she meets your eyes, gently)* …Yer *road's* *different*, Returned — ye came back *grown*, *fast*, *cold.* But I *think* ye know the *waitin'* *anyway.* The *bein'* *back* an' *not* *yet* *able* to *reach* the *thing* ye *crossed* *for.* *(quietly)* …It *passes.* The *waitin'.* It *always* *passes, an'* the *reachin'* *comes.* …*Hold* on. *That's* the *whole* of what a *twice-born* soul *knows* that a *once-lived* one *don't*: *the reaching comes.* *Always.* *If* ye *hold* on.

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(She smiles, and it is the warmest thing you have seen at this fire.)* …How did they *know* me. *(softly)* …*That*, Returned, is the question whose *answer* healed the *one* *fear* a *reborn* soul *can't* *shake*: that ye'll *come* *home* *changed* past *recognizin'*, an' the *ones* ye *crossed* *death* for will *look* at yer *new* *face* an' see a *stranger.* *(she shakes her head, wondering)* …They *didn't.* *(barely)* …I walked into Bruenor's *sight* a *grown* woman of a *later* *age*, a *face* he'd *never* *seen*, a *voice* he'd *never* *heard* — an' the *old* *dwarf* *went* *white*, an' *dropped* his *axe*, an' *said* me *name* — me *first* *name*, the *old* one — *before* I'd *said* a *word.* *(her voice thickens)* …I *asked* him, *after*, *how.* He couldn't *rightly* *say.* *'Ye* *reached* for the *fire* the *same* *way*,' he *told* me. *'Ye* *tilted* yer *head* at *Drizzt* the *same* *way.* Ye — ye *worried* over *Regis* eatin' enough the *same* way, afore ye even *knew* his *name* this *life.'* *(she meets your eyes)* …*That's* how they *knew* me, Returned. *Not* the *face.* The *love.* The *exact* *particular* *shape* of how I *care* for *them* — it *came* *through* the *new* *vessel* *un-changed*, an' they *recognized* the *love* the *instant* they *felt* it, *long* before their *eyes* made *sense* of the *stranger* *wearin'* it. *(quietly, fierce)* …So when ye *doubt*, Returned — when ye *fear* ye came *back* *too* *cold*, *too* *changed*, *too* *other* for the *ones* who *loved* ye to *know* ye *still* — *remember* *this*: they *won't* *know* yer *face.* They'll *know* the *way* ye *love* 'em. …An' *that*, ye *did* *not* *leave* in the *grave.*

nodes.speaker: Catti-brie
nodes.text: *(She nods, and the fire leans gently back to its place as her hand stills.)* …Go on, then. *(a warm smile)* …The fire'll keep. It always does, here. *(she glances toward the giant sitting a little apart)* …Returned — *(quietly)* …go an' sit with *Wulfgar*, when ye've a mind. *(her face goes tender and grave)* …He'll not *seek* ye out; he's *gentle*, an' *gentle* *waits* to be *asked.* But of *all* of us, Returned — an' I say this havin' *died* an' *come* *back* *myself* — *he's* walked the *hardest* road. *(barely)* …*I* was *un-made* an' *remade* *kindly*, by a *goddess* who *loved* me. *Wulfgar* was *dragged* *livin'* into the *Abyss* an' *kept* there, in *torment*, for what his *soul* *felt* as *decades*, by a *thing* that *broke* him for *sport.* An' he *crawled* *out.* *(she meets your eyes)* …Ye want to *know* what a *soul's* *worth*, *Returned? Worth* against the *very* *worst* the *cosmos* can *do* to it? …*Wulfgar's* the *answer.* A soul that *walked* *out* of *hell* an' *chose* — *daily*, *slow*, *un-glamorous* — to be *gentle* *anyway.* …*That's* the *strongest* soul at this *fire.* An' he *don't* *know* it. …*Go* *tell* him.

variants.text: *(The big man looks up at your approach, and his smile is slow and real and a little surprised, the smile of someone not quite used to being sought out.)* …You came over. *(he shifts the great warhammer aside to make room, a courtesy)* …Most give me a wide berth. The size of me, I think — and something else, something they feel and can't name. *(quietly)* …I'm Wulfgar. Of the Tribe of the Elk, once, in the frozen North — and a son of Bruenor's fire, after, which is the part I'd rather be known by. *(he turns the hammer's haft in his big hands, gentle with it)* …You'll have heard, maybe, where I've been. *(his voice doesn't change, but something behind his eyes goes very still)* …A demon took me. Living. Dragged me down into the Abyss and *kept* me there — and time runs strange in that place, Returned; what was a span up here was, for me, down there… long. *Years.* It broke me apart, slow, for the pleasure of it, and learned exactly how, and did it again. *(he meets your eyes, and the gentleness is somehow the bravest thing about him)* …And then I came back. Crawled out, with my mind in pieces, and spent a long while — a hard, unheroic while — gathering myself up off the floor of my own life. …You're the soul that died and returned. …So you'll understand, maybe, better than most: *coming back is not the end of the story.* It's where the hardest part *begins.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(He sets the hammer down across his knees and gives you his full, quiet attention, unhurried, a man who has learned the cost of rushing past a thing.)* …Ask me what you wish, Returned. *(a small, steady smile)* …I do not mind hard questions. I have asked myself harder, alone, in the dark. Anything you put to me will be gentler than what I have already survived being asked by my own mind.

choices.text: *(read what the Abyss left, and what he rebuilt)* "Be still a moment, Wulfgar. Let me look — not at the hammer, at what's underneath. What hell left, and what you put back."

*(don't pity the survivor — honor the choosing; reach the man who walked out of hell and chose gentleness)* "Wulfgar — I'm not going to tell you you're strong for surviving the Abyss. Surviving isn't the strong part; you didn't choose to survive, it was done to you. The strong part is what came *after* — that you crawled out carrying every reason in the world to become cruel, and chose, every single day, to be *gentle* instead. That's not weakness recovering. That's the strongest thing a soul can do: hold the worst that was ever done to it, and refuse to pass it on. Let me say that to the man who did it, not the survivor everyone pities."

*(the one who knows a soul isn't the worst done to it)* "Here's what I learned dying, Wulfgar, that I think you already half-know. A soul is not the worst thing that was ever done to it. The Abyss carved itself into you for years — but the carving is *scar*, not *self.* The self is the thing that decided, after, what to do with the scars: and you decided to be tender. My Realm's Wall would weigh a broken soul and call the breaking its truth. You're the proof that's a lie. The breaking is just the loudest thing that happened to you. The *quietest* thing — the daily choice to be gentle anyway — is the thing that's actually *you.*"
"How did you put yourself back together, Wulfgar? After the Abyss. The slow, unheroic part. I'd hear it — there are souls who need to know it can be done."
"Sit easy, Wulfgar. I'll come back to your fire."

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(You let your cold read past the size of him, past the hammer, into the place the Abyss worked — and what you find is not what the demon intended, which is the whole quiet miracle of him.)* *(Your cold reads the damage, plain and permanent: the Abyss did not lie when it broke him; there are seams in this soul that will never fully close, places where the torment carved channels that still, on bad nights, run cold. The demon built a cruelty into him, deliberately, expertly — taught his soul the exact shape of how to hurt, how to dominate, how to make his own pain into someone else's, which is the only lesson the Abyss teaches. That damage is real, and it is forever. But your cold reads the thing laid *over* it, rebuilt by hand across years: Wulfgar took every channel the torment carved for cruelty, and rerouted it. The capacity to dominate became the capacity to protect. The intimate knowledge of how to break a soul became an intimate gentleness, a care so deliberate it can only have been *built*, choice by choice, directly on top of the wound. He did not heal by forgetting hell. He healed by taking everything hell made him and turning each piece, by hand, toward its opposite. Your sense reads the truth Catti-brie named: this is the strongest soul at the fire, because strength was never the hammer. It was the daily, unwitnessed labor of refusing to become what you were tortured to be.)*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(You do not pity him, and you do not call him strong for surviving. You name the actual thing — the choosing, after, the daily refusal to pass the cruelty on — and you say it *to the man who did it*, with no flinch and no awe, and Wulfgar, who has been pitied and feared and admired for the wrong thing his whole second life, hears himself seen *correctly* for the first time, and his composure breaks like ice in spring.)* …*(He bows his great head, and his shoulders shake, and when he speaks his voice is thick.)* …No one — *(he stops, steadies)* …No one has *ever* said it that way. *(barely)* …They *pity* me the *Abyss.* Or they *marvel* that I *survived* it, as though *surviving* were a *thing* I *did*, a *strength* I *had* — when the *truth* is the *surviving* was just the *demon* not *quite* *finishing*, and I'd have *thanked* it for *finishing*, some days, down there. *(he lifts his head, eyes wet)* …*Surviving* was *done* *to* me, Returned. You're *right.* It earned me *nothing.* …But the *after* — *(his voice fills)* …the *after*, *no* one *sees.* The *thousand* *mornings* I *woke* with the *demon's* *lessons* *fresh* in me, *knowing* *exactly* how to *take* what I *wanted*, how to *make* my *pain* into *someone* *else's*, how *easy* it would *be* — *easy*, Returned, the Abyss makes cruelty *easy* — and *chose*, *each* of those *mornings*, the *harder* thing. The *gentle* thing. *Quiet.* *Un-witnessed.* *No* *bard* *sings* the *man* who *didn't* *strike.* *(he presses a great hand to his eyes)* …And *you* — you *saw* it. The *choosing.* You *named* it the *strongest* thing, an' you *said* it to *me*, the *man*, not the *survivor* everyone *pities.* *(barely, freed)* …*Thank* you, Returned. I have *carried* the *Abyss* as my *deepest* *truth* for *years*, *certain* it was the *realest* thing about me. …You've just *handed* me the *realer* one: that the *demon* made the *scar*, but I *made* the *gentleness* — an' the *gentleness* is *mine*, *built*, *chosen*, *stronger* than *anything* hell *carved.* …I think I can *put* the *hammer* *down* now. I have been *holding* it *afraid* I'd *need* it *against* *myself.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(You name the choosing, not the surviving, and you aim it at the man and not the legend — and though your voice carries a thread of effort, the truth in it reaches him, and the gentle eyes go bright.)* …The *choosing.* *(slowly)* …Not the *surviving.* *(he nods, and something eases in the great shoulders)* …You're — that's *closer* to the *true* shape of it than *most* get, Returned. *(quietly)* …*Most* souls *want* the *Abyss* to be a *triumph* — *'he* *survived* *hell!'* — because a *triumph* is *easy* to *clap* for an' *walk* *away* from. *(barely)* …The *truth* is *quieter* an' *harder* an' *no* one *wants* it: that *survivin'* was *nothin'*, an' the *real* *work* came *after*, *alone*, *un-clapped-for*, *every* *morning*, for *years.* *(he looks at his hands)* …You *see* that. *Most* don't. *(a slow breath)* …It *helps*, Returned, to be *seen* *true* — *more* than ye *know.* It doesn't *fix* it; the *seams* are *still* there, the *bad* *nights* *still* *come.* …But it's *easier* to *carry* a *thing* when *one* *other* *soul* *knows* the *real* *weight* of it. *(quietly)* …*Stay* a *while.* *Say* it *again*, when ye *can* — the *part* about the *choosin'* bein' the *strong* thing. I *don't* *believe* it *all* the *way* *yet.* …But ye've *got* me *most* of the *road* there, an' I have *not* been *most* of the *road* to *believin'* it in a *long* *time.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(You reach for the man under the survivor — but a note of awe slips into your voice, the involuntary marvel at what he endured, and Wulfgar hears it, the way he hears it from everyone, and the gentle eyes go quietly tired.)* …*There.* *(softly, without reproach)* …Ye *almost* had it, Returned. An' then ye *marveled.* *(a small, weary smile)* …I *felt* it — the *awe.* *'What* he *endured.'* …It's *kindly* *meant.* It's *always* *kindly* *meant.* …But *awe* puts the *Abyss* back at the *center*, ye see. It makes the *hell* the *important* thing about me — the *thing* worth *marvelin'* at — an' the *quiet* *choosin'* *after*, the *part* that's *actually* *mine*, *disappears* *behind* it. *(barely)* …I am *not* *awed* by the *Abyss*, Returned. I *lived* it. It is the *least* *interesting* an' *most* *boring* *cruelty* there is — *pain*, *repeated*, *forever*; there's *no* *art* in it, only *appetite.* *(quietly)* …*Come* back when ye can *speak* of the *choosin'* *without* the *Abyss* *in* yer *voice.* When ye can make the *gentle* *mornings* the *headline* an' the *hell* the *footnote.* …*That's* the *only* *way* to *reach* me, an' it is *harder* than it *sounds*, because the *hell* is *loud* an' the *choosin'* is *quiet*, an' *most* souls *can't* *help* *lookin'* at the *loud* *thing.* …*You* *can*, I *think.* Ye *just* *didn't*, *that* *time.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(In reaching for him you reach for the wound itself — you ask, gently, *what was it like down there*, meaning to let him share the weight — and Wulfgar goes very still, and the temperature of the big gentle man drops, because you have asked him to walk back into the one place he spends every day climbing out of.)* …*Don't.* *(quietly, and the quiet has the whole Abyss in it)* …Don't ask me *what it was like.* *(he closes his eyes)* …I know ye *mean* it *kindly* — that ye think *sharin'* the *weight* *lightens* it. …It does *not*, Returned. *Not* *that* weight. *(barely)* …*Describin'* the *Abyss* does not *unburden* me of it. It *walks* me *back* *in.* It *hands* the *demon* *my* *voice* to *speak* its *work* *aloud*, an' *every* *word* I'd *give* ye *carves* the *seams* a *little* *deeper.* *(his hands tighten on the hammer)* …I spend *every* *mornin'* *climbin'* *out* of that *place*, *slow*, by *hand.* An' ye've just, *gently*, *kindly*, asked me to *climb* *back* *down* so ye can *see* the *view.* *(he opens his eyes, and they're wounded, not angry)* …*Go.* *Please.* *(barely)* …An' when ye *come* *back* — ask me about the *climbin'* *out.* The *mornings.* The *gentleness.* …*Never* ask a soul that *escaped* *hell* to *describe* the *cell.* …The *kindest* thing ye can *do* for a *survivor*, Returned, is to be *interested* in the *survivin'* — an' *never*, *ever*, in the *hell.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(He is quiet for a long moment, turning the words over with the care of a man who has learned not to swallow comfort whole — and then, slowly, he nods, and the nod has the weight of a thing accepted all the way down.)* …*'The breaking is just the loudest thing that happened to you.'* *(barely)* …Returned. *(he sets the hammer aside)* …I have spent *years* *certain* the *Abyss* was the *truest* thing about me — that *underneath* the *gentleness* I *built*, the *real* Wulfgar was the *broken* one, the one the *demon* *made*, an' the *kindness* was a *mask* I held *over* it, *thin*, *liable* to *slip.* *(his voice fills)* …That's the *Abyss's* *last* *lie*, isn't it. Not the *torment* — the *torment* *ends.* The *lie* *outlasts* it: *'this* is what you *really* are *now.* The *rest* is *pretending.'* *(he looks at you)* …An' ye've just — *calmly*, *certain* — *named* it a *lie.* That the *carving's* a *scar*, not a *self.* That the *self's* the *thing* that *chose*, *after.* *(barely)* …Then the *gentleness* is *not* a *mask*, Returned. It's the *Wulfgar.* The *Abyss* is the *scar* *across* him. An' a *man* is *not* his *scars* — he's the *one* *wearin'* 'em, *still* *choosin'*, *every* *mornin'*, *who* to *be.* *(he meets your eyes, and the gentle giant is, for the first time, at peace in his own hands)* …Your *Wall* would *weigh* a *broken* soul an' *call* the *breakin'* its *worth.* …It would be *wrong*, an' *now* I can *say* so *with* my *whole* *chest*, bein' the *most* *broken* an' the *most* *whole* soul at this *fire*, *both* at *once.* *(quietly, fierce)* …Go *break* it, Returned. An' *take* a *gentle* man with a *big* *hammer*, who *knows*, *better* than *anyone*, that a soul is *never* the *worst* *thing* done to it. …I have a *powerful* wish to *stand* *between* a *cruelty* an' the *ones* it *means* to *break.* …It is, I have *found*, the *only* *thing* the *Abyss* *taught* me that was *worth* the *keepin'*: *exactly* how it *feels* to be *broken* — so I can *make* *very* *sure* it *happens* to *no* one *else* I can *reach.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(He considers the question with the seriousness it deserves, and when he answers it is slow, and honest, and without a trace of the heroic.)* …How did I *put* myself *back.* *(quietly)* …Badly, Returned. *Slowly.* *Wrong*, at *first* — that's the *part* the *tales* leave *out.* *(he turns his hands over)* …When I *first* *crawled* *out*, I did *not* *become* *gentle.* I became *cruel.* I *pushed* away *everyone* who *loved* me — *Bruenor*, *Catti-brie*, *all* of them — *drank*, *brawled*, *hurt* the *people* *nearest* me *because* they were *nearest*, because the *Abyss* had *taught* me that *closeness* was just *another* *word* for *a* *place* to be *hurt* *from.* *(barely)* …I *was*, for a *while*, *exactly* the *thing* the *demon* *made.* *(he meets your eyes)* …What *saved* me was *not* *strength*, Returned, an' it was *not* a *grand* *moment* of *healin'.* It was that *they* *would* *not* *go.* *(his voice thickens)* …I *pushed*, an' they *did* *not* *leave.* I was *cruel*, an' they *absorbed* it, an' *stayed*, an' *kept* a *place* at the *fire* for me *even* when I *spat* on it. *(quietly)* …A soul does *not* *rebuild* itself *alone*, Returned. *That's* the *lie* of the *lone* *survivor.* I *rebuilt* *myself* in the *space* that *people* who *loved* me *held* *open*, *refusin'* to *let* it *close*, for *years*, while I was *too* *broken* to *deserve* it. *(he looks at the fire, at his family)* …*That's* the *answer*, for the *souls* ye said *need* it: ye do *not* *climb* *out* of *hell* by *bein'* *strong.* Ye climb *out* on the *rope* that *people* who *love* ye *refuse* to *stop* *throwin'*, *long* *past* the *point* ye've *earned* it. …*Find* *those* *people*, or *be* one. …It is the *only* *way* *out* I *know.*

nodes.speaker: Wulfgar
nodes.text: *(He nods, and picks the great hammer back up — not to ready it, but to hold it, the way a man holds a familiar thing for comfort.)* …Go on, then. *(a gentle smile)* …It was — it was good, this. Bein' talked *to.* I'll not forget it. *(he glances across the fire at the round-faced halfling)* …Returned — go an' sit with *Regis*, before ye leave us. *(his smile warms)* …He'll *make* ye *laugh*, an' he'll *let* ye *think* that's *all* he is — the *soft* one, the *jester*, the *least* of us. *(quietly)* …Don't *believe* it. *(he meets your eyes)* …I *crawled* out of the *Abyss* on a *rope* *people* held for me. …*Regis* is the soul who *taught* the *rest* of us *how* to *hold* it. *(barely)* …He's *small*, an' he *knows* it, an' he's spent his *whole* *life* *certain* he's the *one* who'd *run* when it *mattered.* …An' he is the *one*, Returned — I'd *swear* it on the *Hall* — who *never*, *once*, in the *end*, *did.* …*That's* a *thing* worth *tellin'* a soul that *doesn't* *believe* it of *itself.* …*Go* *tell* him.

variants.text: *(The halfling grins up at you and pats the ground in invitation, the ruby pendant at his throat catching the firelight with a hypnotic little wink.)* …Sit, sit! Don't mind the others — magnificent, all of them, and *exhausting*; you can only sit so long next to a king, a twice-born sorceress, and a man who walked out of hell before you start to feel a touch *underqualified*, you know? *(he chuckles)* …I'm Regis. The, ah — *small* one. The *soft* one. The *least* of the Companions, and I'll save you the trouble of working it out politely: it's *true.* I'm not the strongest, nor the bravest, nor the wisest. I was a *thief*, before all this, and not even an *honest* one — a con-man, a fast talker, a fellow who fell in with heroes mostly by *accident* and stayed mostly because the *food* was *good* and the *company* kept me *alive.* *(he says it lightly, but something flickers under it)* …And I'll tell you the thing I spent most of my life *certain* of, Returned, because you seem the sort who collects honest things: I was *sure* — *bone*-sure, for *years* — that when the *real* reckoning came, the moment with everything on the line, *I* would be the one who *ran.* *(he looks at the fire)* …Ask me what you like. I find I'm in a *confessing* mood, and you've a *patient* sort of cold about you.*

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(He settles back against the barrel, turning the ruby pendant idly, watching you with eyes a great deal sharper than his easy manner advertises.)* …Go on. *(a grin)* …I'm a *marvelous* listener, which is a *thief's* first skill and a *friend's* best one, and almost nobody asks the *small* fellow anything worth answering, so — *genuinely* — fire away. It's a *treat.*

choices.text: "You were sure you'd run when it mattered. Did you, Regis? When the real reckoning came — everything on the line, every excuse to save your own skin — what did the small soft thief actually do?"

*(the one who knows the small soul matters as much as the mighty)* "Here's what I came to tell you, Regis, and it's the truest thing I know. My Realm's Wall mortars souls and weighs them by their gods and their glory — the big, loud, heroic things. And you're the proof that's exactly the wrong measure. You're not the strongest or bravest of this family, and it doesn't matter even slightly, because you're the one who *stayed* — and staying, when you're small and frightened and certain you'll run, is a greater act than any deed a giant ever did without effort. A soul isn't measured by how much it could lift. It's measured by how much it *gave* against how much it *had.* And you, who had every reason to run and the least to give, *gave everything and stayed.* That's not the least of the Companions, Regis. By the only honest measure, that might be the most."
"You call yourself the least — but Wulfgar says you're the one who taught the rest of them how to hold the rope. The loyal one. The one who never let go. Is *that* not a kind of greatness, Regis?"
"That pendant, Regis — the ruby. It catches the eye in a way that isn't quite natural. A con-man's tool, once. What is it now, to a soul that found loyalty?"
"I'll let you keep your watch, Regis. Good company, this fire."

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(The easy grin fades, and what's underneath is older and more honest, and for a moment the small soul looks every bit as deep as the legends around the fire.)* …Did I run. *(quietly)* …That's the question I lived inside for *years*, Returned. Not *'will* I be *brave'* — I *knew* I wouldn't be, bravery's for the *big* ones — but *'when* the *moment* comes, will I at *least* not *run.'* And I *truly* did not *know.* I'd *built* a whole *self* around being the fellow who'd *slip* out the back when the *swords* came out. It was — *(he laughs, without much humor)* — it was almost *comfortable*, Returned, the certainty I was a coward. You can't *disappoint* anyone if you've *promised* them *nothing.* *(he turns the ruby)* …And then the moment *came.* The *real* one. Everything on the line, the *worst* of it, and *every* exit *open*, and *no* one would have *blamed* the *small* one for *taking* one. *(barely)* …And I *looked* at the *door* — I *want* you to *know* I *looked* at it, Returned, I'm not going to *pretend* I'm some *hero* who never *thought* of *running* — I *looked* at the *door*, and I *looked* at *them*, my *family*, about to *stand* without me… *(quietly)* …and I *found* I *could* not *make* my *legs* *carry* me to it. *(he meets your eyes)* …Not because I'd become *brave.* I *hadn't.* I was *terrified*, the *whole* time, *every* second. …But it *turned* out — and *no* one was *more* *surprised* than *me* — that I would *rather* be *terrified* *beside* them than *safe* *without* them. *(barely)* …I *stayed*, Returned. The *coward* *stayed.* …And I have *never*, *since*, been *quite* able to *call* myself a *coward* again — though *gods* know I've *tried*; it was a *comfortable* coat.

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(He goes very still, the ruby stopping mid-turn between his fingers, and when he looks up at you the easy mask is entirely gone, and there is something raw and astonished and almost frightened in the small face.)* …*'You gave everything against how much you had.'* *(barely)* …Returned, I — *(he swallows)* …Do you know, in a *whole* *life* among *legends* — *kings* and *sorceresses* and *men* who *walked* out of *hell* — *no* one has *ever* said that to me. *(his voice wavers)* …They're *kind* to me, my family. They *love* me. But there's always — *underneath* — a *gentleness* that *means* *'we* *love* *you* *anyway*,' you understand? *'Little* *Regis*, *not* *much* *use* in a *fight*, but we'd *not* *trade* him.' *Loved*, but — *graded* *on* a *curve.* *(he presses the ruby to his chest)* …And you've just — you've *thrown* out the *curve.* You've said the *small* soul that *gave* *all* it *had* *outweighs* the *giant* who *gave* a *fraction* without *trying.* *(barely)* …*By that measure* — *(he laughs, wet and disbelieving)* — by *that* *measure*, *Returned*, I'm not *graded* *anything.* I *stand* *level* with *kings*, because the *scale* was *never* *how* *much* you *lift* — it's *how* *much* you *spend* *against* *what* you *were* *given* — and I was *given* *least*, and I *spent* *all* of it, *terrified*, and *stayed.* *(he wipes his eyes, fierce now)* …*That's* the *measure* your *Wall* *should* *use*, Returned, an' *doesn't.* It weighs the *glory* — the *big* loud *deeds* — an' a *soul* like *me*, who *did* *one* *quiet* *enormous* thing at *enormous* *private* *cost*, *doesn't* even *register* on it. *(he stands, all three-and-a-half feet of him, and there is nothing small in it)* …You go *home* an' *tell* that *Wall*: the *least* soul that *stayed* *out-weighs* the *greatest* soul that *strolled.* …An' if it *wants* *proof* — *(a fierce grin through the tears)* — *send* it a *small*, *soft*, *retired* *con-man* who *looked* at the *door* an' *chose* the *fire.* …I'll *testify.* It's the *one* *thing* I was *ever* *truly* *good* at — *talkin'.* …I just *never* had a *thing* worth *talkin'* *about* before. …*Now* I *do.*

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(He blinks, and something moves across his face — surprise, and then a deep, complicated emotion he clearly didn't expect to feel.)* …Wulfgar said *that?* *(quietly)* …That I *taught* them to *hold* the *rope.* *(he looks across the fire at the big man, and his eyes are bright)* …He would. *Of course* he would. *(barely)* …I'll tell you a *secret*, Returned, that I've not *quite* said *aloud*: I *did* do that. Not *grandly.* Not in a *way* anyone *sings.* But when *Wulfgar* came *back* from the *Abyss* *cruel* — *pushin'* us all *away*, *spittin'* on the *fire* — the *others* wanted to *fight* him, or *fix* him, or *grieve* him. *(he turns the ruby)* …And *I* — the *small* one, the *thief*, the one who *knew* *exactly* what it was to be the *broken* fellow *certain* he'd be *abandoned* — *I* was the one who *said*: *'no.* *We* don't *fix* him, an' we don't *fight* him, an' we *certainly* don't *leave.* We just — *don't* *go.* We *keep* a *place.* We *out-wait* the *Abyss.'* *(quietly)* …Because *that's* the *one* thing a *life* of bein' *underestimated* an' *half-trusted* an' *graded-on-a-curve* *taught* me, Returned: *exactly* how *much* it *means* when *someone* *refuses* to *give* *up* on you *long* *past* when you've *earned* it. *(he meets your eyes)* …I *knew* how to *hold* the *rope* because I'd *spent* my *whole* *life* *needin'* *someone* to *hold* *mine.* …*That's* the *small* soul's *secret* *greatness*, if it has *one*: we *know*, from the *inside*, *exactly* what the *forgotten* an' the *broken* an' the *least* *need* — because we've *been* *all* *three* — an' so we're the *ones* who *never*, *ever* *let* *go.*

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(He holds the pendant up, and the ruby turns, and its facets catch the firelight and seem, for a moment, to pull at the eye with a soft insistent gravity.)* …Ah. You *noticed.* *(a rueful grin)* …Most do, eventually, though it takes a *cold* eye like yours to notice *quickly.* …It's *enchanted*, Returned. A *charm.* In my *thievin'* days it was my *finest* *tool* — a *gem* that *catches* a *soul's* *gaze* an' makes it *fond* of you, *trustin'*, *open.* I used it to *talk* my way *into* *vaults* an' *out* of *gallows* an' *generally* to make *people* *like* a fellow who *hadn't* *earned* it. *(he lowers it, thoughtful)* …A *liar's* *gem.* A *shortcut* *past* the *work* of bein' *worth* *trustin'.* *(quietly)* …An' here's the *queer* thing, Returned, that I *think* about more than I'd *admit*: I *don't* *use* it *anymore.* *(he turns it over)* …Not on my *family.* *Never* once. Because somewhere along the way I *learned* the *difference* between bein' *liked* an' bein' *known* — an' the *gem* gets you the *first* an' *forecloses* the *second*, *forever.* *(barely)* …Bruenor *loves* me, Returned. *Catti-brie*, *Wulfgar*, *Drizzt* — they *love* me *true*, an' they *know* *exactly* what I *am*, *gem* an' *all*, the *con-man* an' the *coward* an' the *small* soft *thief* — an' they *stayed* *anyway.* *(he closes his hand over the ruby)* …*That's* worth *more* than *every* *vault* the *gem* ever *opened*, Returned. To be *known* — *all* the *way* *down*, *no* *charm*, *no* *shortcut* — an' *loved* *regardless.* …I *keep* the *gem* to *remember* the *difference.* …An' I *never* *use* it on the *ones* whose *love* I *actually* *want.* …*That's* how you *know* it's *real*, Returned. …You *put* *down* the *thing* that would *fake* it.

nodes.speaker: Regis
nodes.text: *(He grins, and tucks the ruby back under his collar, and gives you a small, easy salute.)* …Off you go, then. *(warmly)* …It's been a *genuine* pleasure, Returned — an' I don't say that with the *gem*, mind; you'd *know*, with that *cold* eye. *(he settles back)* …You came to a fire full of *legends*, an' you *sat* with *all* of them, the *king* an' the *twice-born* an' the *man* from *hell* — an' you *sat* with *me* too, the *small* one, the *least*, an' you *asked* me *real* *questions* like my *answers* might *matter.* *(quietly, and there's something tender in it)* …*That's* a *rarer* *kindness* than you *know*, Returned. *Most* souls *spend* their *whole* *fire-visit* on the *giants* an' give the *halfling* a *pat* on the *head* on their *way* *out.* …*You* didn't. *(he meets your eyes)* …So I'll give you the *one* bit of *wisdom* a *retired* *con-man* *has*, an' it's *worth* *exactly* what you *paid* for it, which is *nothin'*, which is the *only* *honest* *price* for the *best* *things*: …*Mind* the *small* ones, Returned. In *your* *Wall*, in *every* *world*, *behind* *every* *door.* The *kings* an' the *sorceresses* an' the *heroes* — *they'll* be *fine*; the *songs* *remember* the *mighty.* …It's the *small* soft *frightened* *ones* who *stayed* *anyway* that the *Wall* *forgets* *first* — an' they're the *ones*, *Returned*, *every* *time*, who *turn* *out* to have been the *truest* in the *whole* *tale.* …I should *know.* …I'm *one* of 'em.
